title: Part 1 — Why measure how an AI agent decides?
summary: If you've put an AI agent in a decision-making seat, you need a way to tell whether it's actually deciding well. Three jobs you can't do without one — and why the obvious shortcuts don't get you there.

title: Part 2 — What a measurement of decision quality has to look like
summary: If you're going to measure how well an AI agent decides, the measurement has to do specific work. Four requirements — and one that quietly rules out most of what's on offer.

title: Part 3 — Is the measurement telling you anything?
summary: A measurement of alignment is only useful if it's meaningful for the decision-maker in front of you. The second layer of the tool is the part that tells you whether the number is evidence or noise.
